chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Product(Product Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier for a product in the TB_PRODUCT table, used to uniquely identify products and join with order line items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | **Description**: Hierarchical product category tree. | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO | | **Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale. | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO | | | PRODUCT_ID | INT | Product ordered | NO | | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master(Cust Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique customer identifier column in the Customer Master table used to uniquely identify customers across related business tables such as orders and shipments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | **Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers. | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO | | **Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses
- Geographic region fo

entities_post_resolution.name: Extended Amount Qty
entities_post_resolution.definition: Line-item total for a product, computed as quantity multiplied by the unit price.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| LINE_AMT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Extended amount (qty × price) | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item.Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The product identifier field of an individual order line item in a sales order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50 | **Description**: Header record for each customer sales order. | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO | | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | **Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├──

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Sales Order is a formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products, including the quantities ordered and unit prices at the time of order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products. | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Customer | **Business Rules**:
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Payment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Payment method (credit card, PayPal, bank transfer)
- Payment amount
- Confirmation timestamp
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, FAILED, REFUNDED | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | Sales O

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a monetary transaction record that settles a specific Sales Order, identified by PAYMENT_ID, and includes payment amount, payment method, timestamp, and status; a Sales Order can have one or more Payments, and each Payment references exactly one Sales Order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Payment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Payment method (credit card, PayPal, bank transfer)
- Payment amount
- Confirmation timestamp
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, FAILED, REFUNDED | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Description**: Monetary transactions settling order amounts. | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | | PAYMENT_METHOD | VARCHAR(50) | Credit card, PayPal, etc. | NO | | | AMOUNT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Payment amount | NO | | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment Confirmation Timestamp
entities_post_resolution.definition: A DATETIME field that records when the payment was confirmed (CONFIRMED_AT).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | CONFIRMED_AT | DATETIME | Payment confirmation timestamp | YES | | | CREATED_AT | DATETIME | Payment creation timestamp | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: A sales order header entity representing the header-level record for a customer order, identified by ORDER_ID and linked to the customer by CUST_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID)

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing a single shipment of goods for a sales order, identified by a unique shipment ID, with origin, destination, ship timestamp, tracking number, and status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Description**: Physical delivery of products to customers. | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | | SHIPPED_AT | DATETIME | Ship timestamp | YES | | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity × Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: Extended amount (line total) calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | | UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price at time of order | NO | | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates

entities_post_resolution.name: Full Refund
entities_post_resolution.definition: Refund is the reimbursement of the purchase price to a customer, issued when an order is canceled or when a product is returned under a merchant's return policy.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: 30 Days
entities_post_resolution.definition: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition:

- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core information about customers including their identity, contact details, regional designation, account creation timestamp, and activation status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, stock-keeping details, naming, category association, pricing, and whether the product is active.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores product category data, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category to represent the category hierarchy.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores high-level information about customer orders, including identifiers, dates, total amount, status, and timestamps for payment confirmation, delivery, and record creation.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores the details of each line item for customer orders, including the product, quantity, unit price, and line total for each order.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments associated with customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for creation and confirmation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments associated with customer orders, including where the shipment originates, when it was shipped, its tracking information, and current status.
